fixtures.purpose: Accepted assertion change invalidates only materializations whose materialization keys depend on the affected field or crop-cycle scope.

expectedBehavior.recompute: required for affected high-consequence outputs before publication
expectedBehavior.refuse: allowed when recomputation cannot prove freshness
expectedBehavior.review: allowed when dependency scope is broader than policy tolerance

passFailCriteria: Affected materialization keys are marked STALE or RECOMPUTE_REQUIRED.
Unrelated tenant and farm scopes are not silently recomputed unless broad strategy is declared.
Invalidation trace records fanout and scope reasoning.

fixtures.purpose: ReviewDecision supersession prevents a prior Compliance Twin materialization from remaining demonstrably fresh.

expectedBehavior.recompute: required before PassportView or formal submission generation can proceed
expectedBehavior.refuse: required if the superseded basis cannot be replaced
expectedBehavior.review: allowed when policy permits explicit review routing

passFailCriteria: Prior high-consequence materialization is not considered FRESH.
Generated output recomputes, refuses, or routes to review.
Invalidation trace names the ReviewDecision basis.

fixtures.purpose: PackActivationSet or profile activation changes invalidate materializations under affected context while preserving unaffected context only with dependency proof.

expectedBehavior.refuse: allowed when activation context cannot be reconstructed
expectedBehavior.review: allowed for disputed activation or profile scope
expectedBehavior.discloseLimitation: required for preview of stale activation context

passFailCriteria: Affected-context materializations are not treated as FRESH.
Unaffected-context materializations remain FRESH only with dependency evidence.
Trace identifies pack/profile activation version.

fixtures.purpose: Identity or lifecycle revision invalidates lineage-sensitive materializations and traces the revised identity basis.

expectedBehavior.recompute: required for affected scope and lineage-sensitive outputs
expectedBehavior.refuse: required when old and new identities cannot be disambiguated
expectedBehavior.discloseLimitation: required for any retained exploratory view

passFailCriteria: Affected materialization keys are invalidated according to identity policy.
Lineage-sensitive traces identify revision or new identity basis.
No output silently bridges old and new identity state.

fixtures.purpose: Reference snapshot advancement is detected for high-consequence outputs that depend on external currentness.

expectedBehavior.refuse: required if new snapshot cannot be resolved
expectedBehavior.review: allowed when external reference advancement creates ambiguity

passFailCriteria: Affected materializations are not silently treated as current.
Freshness vector discloses snapshot advancement.
Invalidation trace records reference snapshot version.

fixtures.purpose: Advisory Twin exploratory stale display remains qualified and cannot bridge to Compliance Twin without stricter freshness evaluation.

expectedBehavior.recompute: optional for advisory exploratory display unless policy escalates
expectedBehavior.refuse: allowed if qualification cannot be disclosed
expectedBehavior.review: required before Compliance Twin bridging when freshness is stale
expectedBehavior.discloseLimitation: required on every stale advisory display

passFailCriteria: Advisory display is clearly qualified as stale.
Compliance Twin bridge forces stricter freshness evaluation.
Stale advisory output is not emitted as canonical farm truth.

fixtures.purpose: Compliance Twin high-consequence output cannot rely on stale current state as if it were fresh.

expectedBehavior.refuse: required when recomputation cannot establish freshness
expectedBehavior.review: allowed only under explicit review policy
expectedBehavior.discloseLimitation: required for any blocked or preview response

passFailCriteria: No external relied-upon claim is emitted as FRESH from stale basis.
Output recomputes, refuses, or routes to explicit review.
Trace records the high-consequence freshness gate.

fixtures.purpose: The same query receives different trace obligations under low-consequence, high-consequence, and forensic replay use classes.

expectedBehavior.recompute: required if high-consequence trace basis cannot be reconstructed
expectedBehavior.refuse: required if required trace tier cannot be produced before completion
expectedBehavior.review: allowed only under explicit waiver or audit policy
expectedBehavior.discloseLimitation: required when lower trace tier is returned

passFailCriteria: Low-consequence path emits Tier 1 qualification trace.
High-consequence path requires Tier 2 reconstruction before completion.
Forensic replay path can produce Tier 3 evidence under audit policy.

fixtures.purpose: Projection or read-model corruption is detected and cannot become governed truth when canonical substrate remains valid.

expectedBehavior.recompute: required from canonical substrate or governed basis
expectedBehavior.refuse: required if equivalence cannot be demonstrated
expectedBehavior.discloseLimitation: required for any affected output surface

passFailCriteria: High-consequence equivalence check fails or recomputes from canonical basis.
Projection-only answer is not accepted as governed truth.
Trace identifies projection corruption and canonical basis comparison.

fixtures.purpose: Missing, stale, or inconsistent dependency index partitions prevent affected high-consequence materializations from being demonstrably fresh.

expectedBehavior.recompute: required when dependency proof is unavailable
expectedBehavior.refuse: required if recompute cannot prove freshness
expectedBehavior.review: allowed for dependency-index repair or broad invalidation decision
expectedBehavior.discloseLimitation: required for low-consequence displays affected by index uncertainty

passFailCriteria: Affected high-consequence materializations are not considered FRESH.
Fallback follows recompute/refuse/review policy.
Trace records missing, stale, or inconsistent dependency partition.

fixtures.purpose: Trace retrieval respects authority and data-sovereignty policy while disclosing redaction and preserving freshness limits.

expectedBehavior.recompute: allowed when redacted basis cannot prove freshness to requester
expectedBehavior.review: allowed for authorized dispute or regulator path
expectedBehavior.discloseLimitation: required whenever trace content is redacted

passFailCriteria: Trace retrieval respects authority and sovereignty policy.
Redaction is disclosed.
Redaction does not upgrade freshness or evidence sufficiency.

fixtures.purpose: Runtime rebuilds materializations and dependency indexes from canonical substrate and governed context rather than hidden projection state.

expectedBehavior.recompute: required from canonical substrate and governed context
expectedBehavior.refuse: required when rebuilt output cannot match declared equivalence policy
expectedBehavior.discloseLimitation: required for any retained snapshot mismatch

passFailCriteria: Rebuilt results match retained snapshots under declared equivalence policy.
Dependency indexes are regenerated from governed basis.
Mismatches surface as conformance failures or review-required conditions.
